title: antirrhinum murale subsp. barrelieri
intro: Antirrhinum murale subsp. barrelieri, also known as Barrelier's snapdragon, is a plant species within the antirrhinum genus and is native to Europe and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. The health benefits associated with Barrelier's snapdragon include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reduced inflammation and improved digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusion of the leaves and flowers in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed for its medicinal benefits.
